name: Human idea Ps2r: preload the next A row-pair while the current row-pair consumes the mirrored B stream

summary.name: Human idea Ps2r: preload the next A row-pair while the current row-pair consumes the mirrored B stream
summary.hypothesis: The B-side mirrored streaming path plus one-fragment lookahead has already collapsed `mio_throttle` to about 0.33, but short scoreboard is still about 6.74 and each row-pair still loads its two A fragments just before issuing the four mirrored column MMAs. A smaller and more targeted follow-up than full panelization is to keep the current B-side path intact and add a one-row-pair A lookahead inside `ptx_wmma_accumulate_row_pairs_64x64`, so the next A fragments are already resident when the current row-pair finishes. That continues the Ps2r family on the operand that has not yet been prefetched within the hot-band micro-tile.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual shared-to-register latency on the A side inside the hot-band row-pair sweep after the B side has already been partially overlapped.
summary.risk: Moderate to high. The implementation is local, but the kernel is already near the register wall, so a second lookahead dimension can erase its own benefit if live fragments grow too much.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if registers rise, if short scoreboard does not fall, or if the hot-band time stays flat.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
